file_insights: Created lib/features/offline/domain/services/conflict_resolver.dart (309 lines): Comprehensive conflict resolution domain layer with enum definitions, data classes, and abstract interface

Created lib/features/offline/infrastructure/sync/conflict_resolver_impl.dart (674 lines): Full implementation of conflict resolution strategies including last-write-wins, server-wins, client-wins, and manual resolution
Modified lib/app/di/modules/offline_module.dart: Registered ConflictResolver as singleton dependency
Modified lib/features/offline/infrastructure/sync/download_sync.dart: Integrated conflict detection and recording during download operations
Modified lib/features/offline/infrastructure/sync/sync_manager_impl.dart: Updated Phase 3 to use ConflictResolver.resolveAllConflicts()
Updated status tracking files: Incremented completed subtasks from 18 to 19, updated session number to 21
Updated implementation_plan.json: Marked subtask 5.4 as completed with comprehensive implementation notes and acceptance criteria verification
patterns_discovered: Offline-first conflict resolution pattern with configurable strategies
Enum-based strategy pattern for conflict resolution (lastWriteWins, serverWins, clientWins, manual)
Domain-Infrastructure separation pattern for conflict resolution services
Entity-specific resolution logic based on conflict types and severities
Conflict logging pattern with persistence support for auditing
Dependency injection pattern for singleton service registration
Conflict detection integration points in sync pipeline (DownloadSync → SyncManager)
Automatic vs manual resolution pattern based on conflict context
Strategy selection based on entity type business rules
gotchas_discovered: Conflict detection requires careful timing (during download when server version is newer but local has pending changes)
Entity-specific resolution rules must balance data integrity with user experience (trips vs journals vs preferences)
Conflict metadata must capture sufficient context for manual resolution scenarios
Integration with existing sync pipeline requires proper injection at multiple points
Conflict logging must be efficient for performance while maintaining audit trail
Strategy configuration needs to be per-entity-type rather than global
Version comparison logic must handle edge cases in timestamp formats and null values
Conflict resolution must preserve data relationships between related entities

approach_outcome.approach_used: Implemented comprehensive conflict resolution system following established project patterns, creating domain layer with strategy enums and interfaces, infrastructure layer with concrete implementations, and proper integration points in sync pipeline
approach_outcome.why_it_worked: Successfully leveraged existing project architecture patterns, implemented comprehensive conflict detection mechanisms, maintained separation of concerns between domain and infrastructure layers, provided configurable strategies per entity type, integrated properly with existing sync manager infrastructure, and documented all acceptance criteria with verification

recommendations: Implement comprehensive unit testing for conflict resolution scenarios including edge cases
Add integration testing between ConflictResolver and SyncManager to verify end-to-end conflict handling
Consider implementing conflict visualization for manual resolution scenarios in the UI
Add conflict resolution metrics and monitoring for production debugging
Implement conflict resolution rollback mechanisms for resolution errors
Add user notifications for automatic conflict resolution actions
Consider implementing conflict prevention strategies where possible
Add database conflict resolution trigger support for additional safety
Implement conflict resolution caching for frequently encountered conflicts
Add conflict resolution history export capabilities for compliance auditing
